page: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-4 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU hardware, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate moderate single-core capabilities alongside weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and integer operations. Single-core performance is average, as demonstrated by a stress-ng single-core score of 1618.89 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1713.0. However, multi-core scaling is relatively weak, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 3813.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 6118.41. Memory bandwidth is also positioned in the lower tier, measuring 28.42 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 21.13 GB/sec for write operations.

Given its hardware profile and benchmark results, the c3-standard-4 is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that do not demand high memory throughput or massive parallelization. It performs adequately in single-threaded tasks, static web serving, and lightweight LLM inference, processing 390.79 tokens per second on a small 135M model. It is less suitable for intensive database operations, software compilation, or heavy multi-threaded computing. Appropriate workloads include general-purpose web serving, basic text processing, and light utility scripting.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-4 is a general-purpose server instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by a 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon 8481C processor on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 2 physical cores with hyper-threading, a 105 MB L3 cache, and support for AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1713.0 and static web serving throughput of 6.68 GB/sec, contrasted by weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth results. This resource profile offers a balanced, qualitative cost-efficiency for moderate, non-parallelized workloads. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, light text processing, and small-scale single-threaded tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-4 is a general-purpose instance with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, offering balanced single-core performance for web serving and light workloads.

tagline: A balanced x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing, optimized for single-threaded tasks and moderate web serving.

Intel Xeon 8481C processor with AVX-512 and AMX extensions
